title: Social Responsiveness Assessment
description: A 65-item assessment measuring social communication and interaction patterns across the autism spectrum. Suitable for ages 2.5 years to adult. Based on published SRS-2 domain structure.

scoring.scale: 1-4 per item (Not true=1, Sometimes true=2, Often true=3, Almost always true=4)

severity_levels.label: mild
severity_levels.description: Mild difficulties — monitoring may be beneficial

severity_levels.label: severe
severity_levels.description: Severe difficulties — comprehensive assessment strongly recommended

social_awareness.description: Ability to pick up on social cues

social_cognition.description: Ability to interpret social cues once picked up

social_motivation.description: Extent of motivation to engage in social-interpersonal behavior

scoring.clinical_note: This assessment provides a T-score profile across five treatment subscales. Elevated scores indicate greater severity of social communication difficulties.

questions.text: Seems much more fidgety in social situations than when alone
Expressions on his/her face don't match what he/she is saying
Seems self-confident when interacting with others
When under stress, shows rigid or inflexible patterns of behavior
Doesn't recognize when others are trying to take advantage of him/her
Would rather be alone than with others
Is aware of what others are thinking or feeling
Behaves in ways that seem strange or bizarre
Clings to adults, seems too dependent on them
Takes things too literally and doesn't get the real meaning of a conversation
Has good self-confidence
Is able to communicate his/her feelings to others
Is awkward in turn-taking interactions with peers
Is not well coordinated
Is able to understand the meaning of other people's tone of voice and facial expressions
Avoids eye contact or has unusual eye contact
Recognizes when something is unfair
Has difficulty making friends, even when trying his/her best
Gets frustrated trying to get ideas across in conversations
Shows unusual sensory interests (e.g., smelling or looking at objects) or unusual ways of playing with toys
Is able to imitate others' actions
Plays appropriately with children his/her age
Does not join group activities unless told to do so
Has difficulty relating to adults
Has trouble keeping up with the flow of a normal conversation
Has difficulty relating to peers
Doesn't respond positively to other people's approaches
Has difficulty with changes in routine
Offers comfort to others when they are sad
Makes unusual eye contact
Has overly serious facial expressions
Is regarded by other children as odd or weird
Becomes upset in a situation with lots of things going on
Can't get his/her mind off something once he/she starts thinking about it
Has good personal hygiene
Is socially awkward, even when trying to be polite
Avoids starting social interactions with peers or adults
Has difficulty understanding the reasons why people do what they do
Has flat or limited emotional responses
Has an unusually narrow range of interests
Has repetitive, odd behaviors such as hand flapping or rocking
Has difficulty understanding group interactions and expectations
Has unusual reactions to sounds, sights, textures, or smells
Responds appropriately to mood changes in others
Focuses his/her attention to where others are looking or listening
Has an unusually loud or flat voice
Walks between two people who are talking
Separates easily from caregivers
Is overly silly or laughs inappropriately
Knows when he/she is talking too loud or making too much noise
Has interests that other people find unusual or strange
Uses words and phrases in an unusual way
Has difficulty following social rules or conventions
Has overly literal understanding of language
Has problems with friendships
Seems anxious or nervous around others
Concentrates too much on parts of things rather than seeing the whole picture
Is emotionally distant, doesn't show his/her feelings
Thinks or talks about the same thing over and over
Is too tense in social settings
Has good eye contact
Has trouble with the concept of personal space
Interacts in unusual ways with peers
Has difficulty understanding humor
Has difficulty reading social cues to guide behavior
